mission.title: clusternet: how to query resources created in child cluster from parent cluster using client-go
mission.description: how to query resources created in child cluster from parent cluster using client-go. This issue affects 0+ users.

steps.title: Identify clusternet feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your clusternet deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=clusternet
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusternet -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: how to query resources created in child cluster from parent cluster using client-go

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant clusternet resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
#### What would you like to be added:

steps.title: Apply the fix for how to query resources created in child cluster from parent
steps.description:
#### Special notes for your reviewer:

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm how to query resources created in child cluster fr is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusternet -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: This issue was resolved by applying the fix from the linked PR. The root cause was identified and addressed by the community.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with clusternet installed or the issue environment reproducible.
